111946.explanation: RVB believes that the College of Mayor and Aldermen should be composed based on suitability. Diversity is a plus but not decisive.

111947.explanation: RVB has already successfully taken the lead to enable the local referendum in the municipality.

111954.explanation: Due to the severe housing shortage, housing seasonal workers on their own company grounds can help address the housing issue.

111959.explanation: Waste collection must always be cost-covering. Everything the municipality does costs money and is always funded through taxes. It is not possible to collect waste for free.

111960.explanation: RVB is against increasing regulatory pressure and bureaucracy. Furthermore, RVB believes that the current standards are sufficient, provided they are properly enforced.

111963.explanation: Sports have an important societal, educational, and social function. RVB values sports and therefore believes sports should be encouraged.

111966.explanation: Although RVB attaches great importance to cultural education, due to budget cuts from the national government to the municipality, there is a structural budget deficit and it is not possible to introduce this subsidy without raising taxes.

111971.explanation: RVB finds enhancing safety important but wants to achieve this through more police presence and visible enforcement on the streets. Installing camera surveillance is too great an infringement on privacy.

111976.explanation: There is currently a housing crisis. RVB supports the legalization of permanent residence in recreational homes where possible. Of course, these holiday homes must be suitable for permanent residence and comply with building regulations.

111978.explanation: Performing socially valuable work not only contributes to society but is also a starting point to help people on welfare get back to work.

111979.explanation: The municipality should help people with low incomes where necessary.

111982.explanation: The municipality should only designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars where there is proven need.

111983.explanation: Creating 30 km/h zones is not a goal in itself; we are not trying to push cars out of the village. We reduce the number of 30 km/h zones and implement them only where necessary, where they improve safety.

111984.explanation: RVB supports the best possible and available care. There should be no distinction based on religion, culture, or belief.

111985.explanation: RVB believes the demand for help is more important than the price. However, youth care must remain affordable.

111986.explanation: Property tax is already high enough. If the budget allows, RVB would like to reduce it.

111988.explanation: The tourist tax in De Ronde Venen is relatively low compared to surrounding municipalities. We can still generate revenue from this that can benefit our municipality.

111991.explanation: RVB supports giving priority to housing applicants from the municipality itself.

111993.explanation: Solar fields can contribute to generating climate-neutral energy if placed in a suitable location, solely to meet provincial requirements. However, the landscape must not be polluted as a result.

111995.explanation: RVB finds school swimming important and has agreed to an initiative to explore the possibilities.

111996.explanation: The function of the coffeeshop is currently being integrated carefully into our community in a way that does not cause nuisance to residents and businesses. There is no need to establish new coffeeshops.

111998.explanation: RVB is proud of the scenic character of our beautiful municipality and is committed to preserving and enhancing it. Therefore, caution must be exercised with large-scale projects, high-rise buildings, or other major developments that could damage this character.

112000.explanation: Currently, at least 30% of newly built homes are social rental homes. RVB supports this.

112002.explanation: The municipality should only install charging stations for electric boats when there is proven demand.

112003.explanation: There is currently a housing crisis. RVB supports the legalization of permanent residence in recreational homes where possible. Of course, these holiday homes must be suitable for permanent residence and comply with building regulations.

111946.explanation: The CDA opposes such a quota. Equal opportunities are important, but appointments should be based on quality and trust, not fixed ratios. Diversity is encouraged by developing talent, not by imposing it.

111947.explanation: The CDA chooses good participation upfront. Referenda simplify complex choices and shift responsibility. Citizens are better involved through early consultation and dialogue, not through overly simple yes/no questions.

111954.explanation: Those who attract labor from outside also have responsibility for good housing. Filling this is up to the entrepreneur themselves, to avoid increasing regulatory pressure on them. The municipality can offer input on this.

111959.explanation: Collecting bulky waste for free sounds attractive but often leads to more waste and higher costs for everyone. We believe it is reasonable for people to take responsibility for their waste. The municipality should organize it so that it remains affordable, accessible, and practical to prevent illegal dumping.

111960.explanation: Current rules already provide sufficient guidance to limit nuisance. Further tightening is unnecessary and would make organizing festivals and parties unnecessarily difficult. With clear agreements and mutual understanding, we keep it pleasant for everyone.

111963.explanation: Sports help people stay healthy and participate in society. For people with welfare benefits, membership fees are often hard to pay. By supporting this selectively, you prevent people from dropping out and help them stay active and involved.

111966.explanation: Music lessons are valuable and enrich children’s development. However, it is not necessary for the municipality to subsidize this for every child. Support should mainly go to families for whom music lessons would otherwise be unaffordable so that everyone can participate.

111971.explanation: Camera surveillance can help prevent and solve crime, especially in places with persistent nuisance. It increases the feeling of safety and supports enforcement, provided it is used carefully and specifically.

111976.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for recreation, not permanent residence. Allowing permanent living causes problems with amenities, safety, and livability, and reduces space for tourism and relaxation. The housing shortage is better solved with real, appropriate housing.

111978.explanation: Participation is important for self-esteem and connectedness. Those who can should have the opportunity to give back to society. Socially meaningful work helps people build structure and take steps forward, with support where needed.

111979.explanation: Municipal taxes are often difficult to pay for people with low incomes. Relief prevents them from falling (further) into debt. The municipality should help those who really need it, so problems do not worsen.

111982.explanation: Electric driving requires sufficient charging points. This can be done well alongside ample parking spaces for fuel cars, for example with combined spots that are used smartly. This keeps parking possible for everyone and prepares us for the future.

111983.explanation: Not every street is the same. A general 30 km/h rule is too simple and does not work everywhere. Where necessary, 30 km/h is allowed, but traffic flow must not stall. Traffic rules should be logical and reflect real-life conditions.

111984.explanation: Care works best when people feel understood and safe. The municipality cannot guarantee everything but should offer room for choice where possible, for example through cooperation and customized solutions. It is a direction, not a rigid guarantee.

111985.explanation: Youth care should focus on good care, not the lowest price. Children and families have the right to help that works and lasts. Focusing too much on costs can lead to constantly changing caregivers and worse care.

111986.explanation: Saying that property tax can be lowered casually is not fair. The municipality must be able to properly perform its tasks and continue investing in amenities such as maintenance, safety, and care. This requires a careful approach, where burdens do not unnecessarily increase but are managed responsibly.

111988.explanation: Tourists contribute to our local economy. A higher tourist tax makes a stay less attractive and affects entrepreneurs. The tourist tax is currently at a reasonable and balanced level: we intend to keep it that way.

111991.explanation: The housing shortage is severe and many people have been waiting for social rental housing for years. Giving priority to one group increases feelings of inequality. The municipality should focus on increasing housing and fair distribution so everyone retains perspective.

111993.explanation: Agricultural land is intended for food production and open landscape. Solar panels belong primarily on roofs, parking lots, and industrial sites. This protects space for agriculture and keeps the landscape open and recognizable.

111995.explanation: Swimming is an important skill, especially in a water-rich municipality. If there is support from schools, the municipality should help make school swimming possible. This way, together we ensure equal opportunities and safety around the water for our children.

111996.explanation: The government must protect people from the risks of drug use. Coffeeshops contribute to health problems, nuisance, and crime. We do not want to normalize soft drugs in everyday life. Therefore, we choose to have as few coffeeshops as possible and no new establishments.

111998.explanation: High residential towers do not fit the village character of De Ronde Venen. We want to build on a scale that suits the environment: perhaps somewhat lower, but thus recognizable. This way, we keep our villages livable and pleasant to live in.

112000.explanation: At least 30% social rental housing in new construction is a good and workable standard. At the same time, we ensure sufficient affordable owner-occupied homes. By preventing improper renting, social rental housing becomes available for those who truly need it, and the housing market keeps moving.

112002.explanation: Electric boating helps make recreation on the lakes cleaner, quieter, and sustainable for the future. By investing in charging stations, the municipality supports entrepreneurs and recreational users in the transition from fossil to electric boating, reducing pressure on nature and the environment.

112003.explanation: Illegal occupation of holiday homes causes problems with safety and livability. Fines must be high enough to prevent violations. At the same time, enforcement must be careful and feasible, so it is clear when there is permanent residence and when it is temporary stay.

111946.explanation: The quality of the executive board is our top priority.

111947.explanation: We are against a referendum because important choices for our municipality cannot be answered with a simple yes or no.

111954.explanation: Good housing for seasonal workers is important and must be organized appropriately. This can also be at a location other than the company's own premises.

111959.explanation: Nothing is free. If bulky waste is collected for free, it is paid for by other residents.

111960.explanation: Organizations, entrepreneurs, and residents must be given the space to organize events. This requires fewer rules, not more.

111963.explanation: That is already happening and we support it. However, maximum effort must be put into getting people out of social assistance and into work.

111966.explanation: This is a responsibility of the parents.

111971.explanation: Camera surveillance must be used more often to prevent crime and nuisance.

111976.explanation: We want to look, in consultation with the province, at which homes can qualify for permanent residence. Those homes must then meet the requirements.

111978.explanation: People on social assistance should be helped into work. If that is not yet possible, socially meaningful work is a good option in exchange for social assistance benefits and to get into the work process.

111979.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes should only be possible for people who really need it.

111982.explanation: We support installing charging points based on the growth in the number of electric cars. This way, we ensure sufficient parking spaces for both non-electric and electric cars.

111983.explanation: The maximum speed limit should only be lowered to 30 km/h in places where it is necessary for traffic safety.

111984.explanation: We support good and appropriate care, regardless of faith, culture, or belief.

111985.explanation: The costs of youth care are high, but effective care is also important to prevent future costs. Therefore, attention must be paid to the right balance between price and quality.

111986.explanation: The government must remain strict on expenditures and keep taxes as low as possible.

111988.explanation: Raising taxes is not a goal in itself. A higher tourist tax negatively impacts the number of tourists and our local economy.

111991.explanation: Many young people and residents have been waiting for housing for years. Giving extra priority to refugees is not fair. As a municipality, we meet our legal obligation for housing status holders.

111993.explanation: In the next four years, there is no possibility to connect solar fields to the power grid. Therefore, the focus in the coming years lies on insulation, saving energy, and innovation.

111995.explanation: Swimming lessons are a responsibility of the parents. Arrangements are available to organize swimming lessons for vulnerable groups.

111996.explanation: A coffeeshop reduces illegal drug trade on the streets and in the neighborhood. When there is demand and the rules are met, a new coffeeshop is a possibility.

111998.explanation: In suitable locations, we should consider apartment complexes that fit our villages. Think, for example, of the apartments in Maricken in Wilnis. Some residents prefer a balcony over a garden. High-rise buildings also make it possible to build more homes.

112000.explanation: We support more affordable owner-occupied homes in De Ronde Venen. Everyone should have a chance to buy a home.

112002.explanation: Installing charging stations for electric boats should be possible, but placing them is not the municipality's responsibility.

112003.explanation: The municipality should look into the possible legalization of holiday homes and enforce regulations where legalization is not possible.

111946.explanation: D66 stands for equal opportunities and a government that reflects society. We strive for diversity in the executive board so that different perspectives are included at the table.

111947.explanation: Residents must have direct influence on their living environment. For clear choices, we organize referenda, both municipality-wide and per village. This way, we give residents a voice that goes beyond voting once every four years.

111954.explanation: Migrant workers are indispensable for our economy and deserve decent housing. D66 permits housing on own premises, provided the quality is good and no nuisance is caused.

111959.explanation: We make sharp choices and do not take on projects where costs and social benefits are not proportionate. Responsible use of public funds means every euro we spend must contribute to the livability and quality of our eight villages.

111960.explanation: We are not aware of any examples where this causes problems in the municipality.

111963.explanation: Everyone should be able to play sports, regardless of financial means. D66 supports the Green Heart Pass, so that people with low incomes can become members for free or at a discount.

111966.explanation: Cultural education enriches your world. D66 wants culture and music to have a permanent place at school, so every child comes into contact with it, not just children whose parents can afford it.

111971.explanation: Safety is important. We use camera surveillance where it demonstrably contributes to safety. Privacy is always a significant concern for D66.

111976.explanation: In times of housing shortage, we must be practical. D66 wants to allow permanent residence in recreational homes, provided the home is safe and complies with building and parking regulations.

111978.explanation: D66 is based on trust. We encourage people to engage in work or volunteer work because participation makes people happier. A mandatory reciprocal obligation as part of customized support must remain possible.

111979.explanation: Absolutely not. For residents who can barely make ends meet, debt relief is essential. D66 will not let people in poverty down.

111982.explanation: Electric driving is the future. D66 ensures sufficient charging points in every village, also for shared cars. This way we make clean transport easy and accessible for everyone.

111983.explanation: D66 makes 30 km/h the standard speed in residential areas. This way, the street becomes for cyclists and pedestrians again. Only on the major through roads do we maintain 50 km/h for accessibility and emergency services.

111984.explanation: Care is human work and must fit the individual. D66 stands for customized care. Residents should feel understood and at home with their caregiver. That increases the chance that the care will succeed.

111985.explanation: When caring for our vulnerable youth, price should not be the leading factor. D66 chooses quality and trust in the professional. Cheap care often turns out to be costly if problems are not truly solved.

111986.explanation: D66 wants a reliable government that maintains its services. Lowering the property tax means cutting back on sports, culture, or care. We do not want that. We strive for stable charges, not destructive policies.

111988.explanation: Visitors enjoy our amenities and nature; it is reasonable that they contribute to that. D66 wants to align tourist tax with surrounding municipalities so that the burden does not rest only on our own residents.

111991.explanation: D66 follows the law and takes its responsibility for status holders. However, we primarily want to build more so that 'who gets priority' is not a conflict between groups of housing seekers.

111993.explanation: A total ban goes too far, but the priority is clear: first fill the roofs and parking lots, then the land. We only allow solar fields on agricultural land if it is really unavoidable, and residents must benefit as well.

111995.explanation: In a water-rich municipality like ours, swimming ability is vital. D66 wants every child to obtain their diploma. We have safety nets through poverty policy for those who cannot afford it. Swimming as a sports activity during school hours should be facilitated.

111996.explanation: D66 is in favor of regulated sales to tackle the backdoor problem. We are not necessarily against a coffeeshop, provided this happens safely and under control and keeps crime out.

111998.explanation: We encourage appropriate high-rise buildings close to amenities. Examples include the passage in Mijdrecht and the apartment complex at Abcoudermeer.

112000.explanation: To keep our villages affordable for everyone, D66 adheres to the norm of 30% social rental housing in new construction projects. Owning a home should not be a privilege for the lucky few.

112002.explanation: Mainly, D66 sees this as the responsibility of the marinas. If it proves impossible to create a viable business case, the municipality can provide subsidy schemes.

112003.explanation: Enforcing rules against people just looking for a roof over their heads does not solve the housing shortage. D66 wants to legalize permanent residence where possible, instead of pushing residents out with high fines.

111946.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that local government reflects the local community well. Therefore, it would be great if at least 50% of the executive board consists of women. At the same time, this also depends on candidates put forward by factions.

111947.explanation: It is possible to hold a referendum in De Ronde Venen and GroenLinks-PvdA wants to keep it that way. However, a referendum is not our preference because most questions cannot be captured in a simple yes/no question. GroenLinks-PvdA prefers to use citizens' assemblies instead.

111954.explanation: It is important that employers take responsibility to house their seasonal workers decently and safely. However, in De Ronde Venen there is very limited, if any, seasonal labor.

111959.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to encourage residents to separate their waste as well as possible. This allows the materials to be reused optimally. This also means that it must be easy to dispose of your waste without unnecessary obstacles.

111960.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that residents do not experience nuisance from large parties and festivals in the municipality. Current noise standards already apply and must be enforced if there is a nuisance. If standards prove insufficient, we want to tighten them.

111963.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA thinks it is important that everyone can participate in the municipality. Therefore, we continue the existing scheme for children from low-income families to participate in sports, art, and culture.

111966.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that everyone can participate in the municipality. Therefore, we maintain the existing scheme to allow children from low-income families to take part in sports, art, and culture.

111971.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes camera surveillance should always be used purposefully, for example in places with problems. We are not in favor of generic increased camera surveillance, but we do not exclude an increase where justified.

111976.explanation: Holiday homes are meant for recreation, not permanent residence. At the same time, holiday homes meet a housing need due to the housing shortage. This can justify converting a holiday home into a regular residence. This is always assessed on a case-by-case basis.

111978.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that everyone can participate in society. If that is not possible through work and social assistance is needed, we do not believe a reciprocal obligation should apply. We want to work together to see what someone can do and encourage that.

111979.explanation: Residents with a low income have little spending money and face financial problems without debt relief. Therefore, GroenLinks-PvdA supports the possibility of debt relief for households that cannot otherwise pay their taxes.

111982.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that the transition to increasing electric transport proceeds smoothly. For this, more public charging stations are needed in the coming years.

111983.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports 30 km/h speed limits in neighborhoods. Lowering the maximum speed makes traffic safer in our municipality. For pedestrians, cycling children, and elderly with mobility problems, this makes a huge difference. Streets also become quieter and the air cleaner.

111984.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA values freedom of choice in care. Care providers must meet sufficient standards. Within that, residents should be free to choose the caregiver that suits them.

111985.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that good care is provided. If that costs more, that is not a problem because it prevents problems from being poorly resolved or worsening. If the municipality only focuses on price, cheap care is ultimately expensive.

111986.explanation: The property tax is an important source of revenue for the municipality. It allows us to maintain amenities such as community centers, swimming pools, and libraries. It also enables investment in schools, roads, and greenery. Lowering property tax is only possible if costs decrease.

111988.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA does not believe taxes must by definition go up or down. It depends on the expenses of the municipality and whether additional income is needed. We are therefore against a generic further increase in tourist tax.

111991.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that refugees with residence permits can participate as soon as possible. Housing is crucial for this. Municipalities are obliged to house a certain number of status holders annually. The municipality has done this for years and will continue to do so.

111993.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that together we achieve climate goals. We maximize rooftop solar, but energy generation on the land remains necessary. This can only be done with wind turbines and solar fields. Excluding these makes achieving climate goals impossible.

111995.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports school swimming. However, experience shows schools see little value in it because of challenges with transport and supervision. If schools want to participate, we would like the municipality to contribute.

111996.explanation: De Ronde Venen has a coffeeshop in Mijdrecht. GroenLinks-PvdA thinks it may remain. We see no need for a second coffeeshop but will not block its arrival.

111998.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important to handle our space carefully and to build enough new homes. Therefore, we think building upwards should be considered more often, as long as it fits the village character.

112000.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants more affordable housing. We want 70% of all new homes to be affordable, with at least 40% of those being social rental. Currently, about 27% of homes in De Ronde Venen are social rentals, while about 35% of residents qualify.

112002.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports quiet water recreation such as sailing, rowing, stand-up paddling, and electric boating. It is important that there are opportunities to charge your boat; recently, a motion initiated by GroenLinks-PvdA was adopted to this effect.

112003.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that recreational homes should not be permanently inhabited. After all, they are not intended for that purpose. However, due to the housing shortage, people may be forced to do so. Increasing fines on illegal habitation does not contribute to a solution.

111946.explanation: The Seniorenpartij cares that the best people are in the right positions. If this were all women, we would be fine with that. And vice versa!

111947.explanation: The municipal council is elected by the residents, who give the municipal government their mandate. We consider it a vote of no confidence against the municipal government if a referendum is held for every important decision.

111954.explanation: For people from abroad, this is really necessary.

111959.explanation: Collecting trash costs money. Then cuts have to be made elsewhere. It is a small effort to bring this to the waste disposal site.

111960.explanation: It’s nice if a party or festival can be organized for certain groups, but not everyone should experience nuisance because of it.

111963.explanation: If people really do not have enough income to pay for a sports club membership, the municipality should step in. This is already happening, by the way.

111966.explanation: Making music and talent development should be encouraged, even if parents are unable to pay for it.

111971.explanation: If necessary, it can be allowed in certain places within the municipality. Possibly temporarily.

111976.explanation: That is a difficult problem. Holiday homes often do not meet the requirements that a permanent home must meet. Moreover, the infrastructure is also insufficient.

111978.explanation: The Seniorenpartij does not like forced measures. It should come from the recipients of social assistance themselves. After all, they receive money from the community, so they can be expected to give something back.

111979.explanation: It should not be automatic that if you have a low income, you do not have to pay municipal taxes.

111982.explanation: More and more electric cars are desired, so extra charging stations must also be installed.

111983.explanation: But only in the built-up centers of the villages. Outside those areas, it should be higher.

111984.explanation: That would be nice, but in practice it is not feasible. There are already too few people in healthcare. Moreover, the wishes of those being cared for are always taken into account.

111985.explanation: Youth care is very expensive, so it is good to pay attention to the costs.

111986.explanation: Unfortunately, the property tax is necessary to continue doing certain things in the municipality. Moreover, it is relatively low compared to similar municipalities.

111988.explanation: We should benefit more from the tourists who visit our municipality.

111993.explanation: This is the alternative to not wanting to have wind turbines. It must remain within certain limits.

111995.explanation: School swimming would be nice, but the costs should not be entirely borne by the municipality. Private swimming lessons also cost money, so parents should contribute as well.

111998.explanation: We agree with the previously agreed maximum height of 5 stories. We think that is high enough. After all, De Ronde Venen is an agricultural municipality and not a big city. That would pollute our horizon.

112000.explanation: That has already been decided in DRV.

112002.explanation: Good plan, but not solely at the expense of the municipality.

111946.explanation: The quality of the leader is more important than whether it is a man or a woman. The statement says "must."

111947.explanation: According to us, there are better ways to gather the opinion of residents. Moreover, a referendum is a yes/no question without nuance or discussion about for or against.

111954.explanation: Municipality points out entrepreneurs’ responsibilities regarding proper housing (text VP2026).

111959.explanation: In the past, it was deliberately chosen that people bring their waste themselves, which automatically limits the waste stream. Free collection would only increase the waste pile.

111960.explanation: We notice that residents, especially in the centers, sometimes experience quite a lot of nuisance and there is also a risk of hearing damage.

111963.explanation: Not a must, but there are arrangements that provide for this.

111966.explanation: It is primarily the parents' responsibility. If they have no money for it, they can make use of the existing arrangements.

111971.explanation: Not as a starting point, no unless.

111976.explanation: Acceptable as an emergency measure due to the housing shortage, provided the conditions are well defined.

111978.explanation: It is important that people participate.

111979.explanation: There are situations where this is important, also as part of debt restructuring.

111982.explanation: Increasing the number of charging points will stimulate the growth of electric cars.

111983.explanation: Main roads generally at 50 km/h, but where possible, it may also be 30 km/h.

111984.explanation: Basically agree, provided the quality of the care provider is sufficient.

111985.explanation: Quality of care is more important and can ultimately even be cheaper (less effort, faster effect).

111986.explanation: This municipality is not expensive in the benchmark and reduction would immediately mean a deterioration of facilities.

111988.explanation: Already significantly increased in recent years.

111991.explanation: The instrument must be maintained because it is necessary to comply with the legal task and to relieve the central asylum seekers' center, for example in Ter Apel.

111993.explanation: An absolute ban goes too far, but there may be locations where it is well applicable, whereby we find it important that the agricultural destination remains (dual zoning). We are also in favor of a good mix of solar and wind power.

111995.explanation: In our opinion, schools do not want this. It is complex and costly to implement. It is better to support parents with swimming lessons where necessary.

111996.explanation: As far as we are concerned, we stop drug trafficking in our municipality.

111998.explanation: Although the question remains what "high" means. But if it fits the image, we are not against it here.

112000.explanation: Where we also want more space for mid-range rent, which is now in great shortage.

112002.explanation: No, the boat rental companies and marinas, respectively the owners of berths, should do that.

112003.explanation: Where possible, we want to grant temporary personal exemptions and thus provide space. Where this is not possible or people do not want this, clear enforcement should follow. This is explicitly stated in our election program. So on the one hand cooperate on expansion, but on the other hand also act more strictly.

parties.name: Lokaal en Fair De Ronde Venen
parties.fullName: Lokaal en Fair De Ronde Venen

111946.explanation: Lokaal en Fair is committed to policies in which diversity has a place.

111947.explanation: Lokaal en Fair is cautious about holding a referendum because many plans cannot be answered with a yes or no. We want genuine participation, in which residents can think along and participate in time.

111954.explanation: Employers must accommodate foreign seasonal workers as much as possible on their own company premises, whereby the housing is safe, healthy, and livable.

111959.explanation: A small contribution will still be asked for bulky waste collection.

111960.explanation: Neighborhood parties, village barbecues, open days at associations, and local activities connect residents and are given space.

111963.explanation: For young people from low-income parents, there are many opportunities to play sports for free and participate in culture.

111966.explanation: Lokaal en Fair wants to expand the out-of-school offer with technology, programming, music, and writing, in collaboration with entrepreneurs and associations.

111971.explanation: Lokaal en Fair wants more CCTV where necessary on industrial estates and near shopping centers. Every resident must feel safe, at home and on the street.

111976.explanation: Lokaal en Fair wants to start a pilot for permanent residence in recreational homes, provided there are sufficient utilities (sewage, water, and electricity).

111978.explanation: Anyone using social assistance or poverty support must show that they are working hard to get a job as soon as possible and actively participate in society. Lokaal en Fair commits to halving the number of people on social assistance within two years.

111979.explanation: Poverty support is generous and mainly temporary. Think of waiving municipal taxes for low-income earners, special assistance for unexpected expenses, and support for the Food Bank and clothing bank.

111982.explanation: Lokaal en Fair wants to place charging stations as needed in all villages.

111983.explanation: Lokaal en Fair wants 50 km/h roads where appropriate, but 30 km/h where necessary to increase safety. Enforcement should take place at both speeds.

111984.explanation: The number of care providers will be limited; they are responsible for any subcontractors. These may not make a profit and will be more closely monitored for efficiency and quality execution. Care must be available within reachable distance.

111985.explanation: The number of youth assistance providers will be limited; they are responsible for any subcontractors. These may not make a profit and will be more closely monitored for efficiency and quality execution. Youth assistance must be available within reachable distance.

111986.explanation: Keep local taxes (such as property tax, fees, and sewage charges) moderate. If extra money is needed, we first check if it can be rearranged within existing resources. Vulnerable groups will be spared in this.

111988.explanation: We keep local taxes (such as property tax, fees, and sewage charges) moderate. If extra money is needed, we first check if it can be rearranged within existing resources. Vulnerable groups will be spared in this.

111991.explanation: Lokaal en Fair wants priority for residents of De Ronde Venen for affordable rental and owner-occupied homes.

111993.explanation: Lokaal en Fair focuses on more solar panels on roofs, only then on meadows. No tall wind turbines and large-scale solar fields near villages and near Angstel and Gein.

111995.explanation: Every child must have the opportunity to learn to swim. Parents who cannot afford swimming lessons will receive full reimbursement.

111996.explanation: No new coffeeshops will be opened in De Ronde Venen.

111998.explanation: Lokaal en Fair focuses on preserving the village character, which is also reflected in the design and height of buildings.

112000.explanation: Lokaal en Fair has long been committed to 1/3 social rental homes, 1/3 affordable owner-occupied homes in new housing projects.

112002.explanation: Companies are responsible for their own facilities such as charging stations.

112003.explanation: Lokaal en Fair wants to start a pilot for permanent residence in recreational homes, provided there are sufficient utilities (sewage, water, and electricity).

111946.explanation: The most important thing is that the right people are in the right place and whether that is a man or a woman cannot and should not be an issue.

111947.explanation: The municipal council represents the residents; referenda are therefore not necessary.

111954.explanation: Employers in agriculture and horticulture must provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises. Foreign workers are welcome if no local staff can be found, but our residents must not be disadvantaged in the housing market.

111959.explanation: Free home collection can actually lead to more litter. The current policy works and should therefore be continued, so that we do not incur higher costs.

111960.explanation: That one time per year cannot lead to a ban, but in other cases, comply with national standards.

111963.explanation: People receiving social assistance benefits can receive a certain allowance through the municipality. Currently, this happens via the Groene Hart Pass.

111966.explanation: We prefer music lessons at schools. Through the Groene Hart Pass, children receive reimbursements to be able to do these kinds of activities.

111971.explanation: More camera surveillance increases safety, especially around schools. Those who have nothing to hide should not have any problems with this.

111976.explanation: It is permitted to use a recreational home temporarily as a main residence, as long as the occupant actually lives there. Upon departure, the home reverts to recreational use because it may not become a regular dwelling.

111978.explanation: Volunteering for 10 to 20 hours a week keeps people active, increases their experience, and offers better chances to transition to regular work.

111979.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes helps low-income residents to stay financially afloat.

111982.explanation: Closely monitor developments to see how many charging stations are needed. Or wait until residents request an extra charging station in their area and then respond accordingly.

111983.explanation: In residential and living areas, reducing the speed limit to 30 km/h is desirable and effective for safety. However, through roads within villages serve a different purpose: they are meant to allow traffic to flow smoothly.

111984.explanation: Everyone deserves care that aligns with their beliefs, but this is only feasible when there is enough staff to meet these preferences.

111985.explanation: The most important thing is the final result, although price is also an important factor.

111986.explanation: Do not lower, but also do not raise. Keep it as it is now. Follow the inflation trend.

111988.explanation: Our municipality has beautiful nature that needs proper maintenance. An increase in tourist tax helps make this possible, so tourists can optimally enjoy our surroundings.

111991.explanation: The municipality must first be able to provide good, affordable housing for its own residents.

111993.explanation: If the farmer wants this on his meadow, that option should be available.

111995.explanation: We live in a water-rich area, so it is very important that children (especially from different countries) can swim. Currently, this is not available to every child.

111996.explanation: Prefer controlled over uncontrolled sale. The current coffee shop does not cause any nuisance.

111998.explanation: High-rise buildings are acceptable up to a maximum of 7 floors and preferably at the edges of villages.

112000.explanation: Our municipality scores better than the national average with 30% social rental housing compared to 28%. It is important that this ratio is maintained in future housing development.

112002.explanation: Municipal investment in charging stations for boats only makes sense in accessible locations; at marinas, owners should provide their own facilities.

112003.explanation: The fine amount between €435 and €5000 is already high enough.

statements.title: The municipality should invest money in charging stations for electric boats.
There should be more camera surveillance in De Ronde Venen.

titleParts.text: In De Ronde Venen moet meer

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: More high-rise residential buildings should be allowed in De Ronde Venen.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent the opening of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: At least 30% of the newly built homes in De Ronde Venen must be social rental housing. Currently, 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in De Ronde Venen moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The residents of De Ronde Venen should be able to express their opinions on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van De Ronde Venen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Fines for illegally living in a holiday home should be increased.
